question.text: Under the SEBI (Intermediaries) Regulations, 2008, the intermediary must provide SEBI with a compliance certificate on which date each year?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** As per Regulation 12 of the SEBI (Intermediaries) Regulations 2008, the intermediary is required to provide SEBI with a certificate of its compliance officer on the 1st April of each year. **The Trap:** March 31 is the financial year-end, but the compliance certificate is due on April 1, which is the first day of the new financial year. **Cross-Exam Context:** This date is specifically tested in NISM Series III-A compliance chapters. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The certificate must confirm compliance with all obligations and that all disclosures in Form A are true and complete.
